name: Seafood Croquettes
recipeIngredient: 1 pound Cooked fish and/or shellfish (such as salmon, shrimp, scallops, white fish, or a combination)
recipeInstructions:
1. Melt the butter in a small skillet over medium heat.
2. Add the onion and celery and cook, stirring occasionally, for 5 minutes or until soft.
3. Add the flour, stirring to form a paste.
4. Add the milk and nutmeg and cook another minute or so until the mixture thickens.
5. In a separate plate, place bread crumbs.
6. Scrape the mixture into a mixing bowl and add the fish, parsley, chives, salt, and pepper.
7. Using two forks, mash the fish into the mixture.
8. Drop tablespoon-sized dollops of the mixture into the bread crumbs.
9. Roll each dollop in bread crumbs, then between the palms of your hands to form balls.
10. Set croquettes aside on a plate while preparing the oil for frying.
11. Fill a medium-sized cast iron or other heavy skillet with 1 1/2 inches of cooking oil and place over medium heat on the stove.
12. Heat the oil until it reaches a frying temperature of 350°F (175°C). You can test this by dropping a small amount of water into the oil; if it dances and evaporates quickly, the oil is hot enough.
13. Carefully drop croquettes into the hot oil in batches if necessary, ensuring they are not overcrowded.
14. Fry croquettes until golden brown, about 3 minutes.
15. Using a slotted spoon, remove croquettes from the oil and drain on paper towels.
16. Arrange the croquettes on a linen napkin and serve immediately with tomato mayonnaise.
To Freeze:
1. Place frozen uncooked croquettes on a lightly oiled cookie sheet and place in the freezer until solid.
2. Transfer the frozen croquettes to an airtight plastic bag or lidded container.
3. Cook directly from the freezer, increasing cooking time by about 5 minutes.
To Freeze Cooked Croquettes:
1. Cool cooked croquettes completely at room temperature.
2. Place them in an airtight plastic freezer bag or lidded container.
3. Label and place in the freezer for up to 3 months.
4. To cook, place frozen cooked croquettes on a cookie sheet and heat in a preheated oven at 375°F (190°C) for 20 minutes.
